title: Dichodon cerastoides
taxonomy: Dichodon cerastoides is a genus of extinct plants that belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida, which is a group of ancient vascular plants. This classification is within the division Lycopodiophyta, a group of plants that have vascular tissues but lack true leaves and flowers. Lycopodiophyta is further classified under the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Dichodon cerastoides also includes being a member of the family Rhyniaceae, a group of extinct plants that date back to the Silurian period. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary history and relationships of this ancient plant species.
